generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically retrieve and refresh all Event and Activity data from both Sync Matrix 1.0 and Exercise Management Tool 1.0 on a fixed 60-second cycle.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-01 and Business Objective 1 mandate a 60-second automated refresh cycle to ensure real-time situational awareness.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow an authorized user to manually pause the automatic 60-second data refresh cycle and resume it on demand, after which the 60-second cycle shall restart from the moment of resumption.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-01 explicitly permits manual pause and mandates cycle resumption upon user command to support briefing moments.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display a Multi-Document Interface (MDI) layout in which the left two-thirds of the application window is permanently allocated to a graphical timeline view and the right one-third is permanently allocated to a data table view; this ratio shall not be user-configurable.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-02 fixes the display layout proportions to ensure consistent projection-ready presentation.

generated_requirements.text: Within the data table region, the top one-quarter shall display sequential Events (order-dependent, not time-bound) and the bottom three-quarters shall display Events ordered by temporal proximity to the current exercise time.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-03 defines the segmentation of the table region to separate sequential from temporally relevant Events.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall render a graphical timeline in the left-panel region that plots Events and Activities as nodes positioned according to their scheduled exercise time, with the current exercise time indicated by a visible marker on the timeline axis.
generated_requirements.rationale: The graphical timeline is the primary situational awareness artifact described in the Future State and Scope sections.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall color-code each displayed Event and Activity based on its variance from the current exercise time, using color thresholds (e.g., on-time, at-risk, overdue) that conform to the DHS color schema standard.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-04 and Business Objective 2 require color-coded schedule variance indicators to enable rapid visual triage.

generated_requirements.text: All visual elements of the application — including backgrounds, text, highlights, borders, and status indicators — shall conform to the Department of Homeland Security color schema; no custom or ad hoc color choices are permitted without formal DHS approval.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-05 and RC-01 mandate strict adherence to the DHS color schema across all UI elements.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display two independent, simultaneously visible clocks at all times: one showing current local (wall-clock) time and one showing current exercise time, each visually distinct from the other.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-09 requires dual-clock display to eliminate confusion when exercise time diverges from local time.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a direct deep-link from each displayed Event or Activity record to its corresponding record in the originating source application (Sync Matrix 1.0 or EMT 1.0), enabling the user to open the source record for modification without leaving the EEMDS display context.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-06 prohibits editing within EEMDS and requires seamless navigation back to source applications for all modifications.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display only MSEL Summary data for Events and Activities in both the graphical timeline and the data table; full MSEL detail records shall not be rendered within EEMDS.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-07 restricts the data scope to MSEL Summary data to keep the display concise and operationally relevant.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow authorized users to filter the displayed Events and Activities by Venue, returning only Events associated with the selected venue(s).
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-11 mandates venue-based filtering to reduce cognitive load during fast-moving exercise scenarios.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow authorized users to filter the displayed Events and Activities by Key Events designation, returning only Events flagged as key events.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-11 mandates key-event-category filtering as a second required filter dimension.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support simultaneous application of both the Venue filter and the Key Events filter, returning only Events that satisfy all active filter criteria.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-11 explicitly states that filters may be applied independently or in combination.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide an on-demand 'Full Exercise View' that clears all active filters and displays all Events and Activities in the exercise, accessible at any time regardless of which filters are currently applied.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-13 requires an unfiltered view of all exercise Events to be accessible on demand.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow authorized users to define, name, and configure time segments used to organize Events on the timeline; these segments shall be configurable per exercise and may be set or modified prior to or during exercise execution.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-10 requires user-defined time segments to be configurable per exercise for meaningful timeline organization.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall generate and maintain a history report that captures all changes to Event and Activity data — as reflected in EEMDS from source systems — including the field changed, previous value, new value, timestamp of change, and source application; this report shall be retrievable at any point during or after the exercise.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-12 and Business Objective 6 require a complete, retrievable audit trail to support post-exercise after-action review.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall produce an Actual versus Assumed Time variance chart for Events and Activities, quantifying the difference between the scheduled (assumed) time and the actual execution time for each record.
generated_requirements.rationale: The Scope section lists actual versus assumed time variance charts as an in-scope deliverable for quantitative variance analysis.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall authenticate every user attempting to access EEMDS and shall deny access to any user who is not on the pre-authorized user list maintained by the system administrator; no guest or anonymous access modes shall be supported.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-08 and Business Objective 7 require positive authentication and denial of all unauthorized access attempts.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall integrate with Sync Matrix 1.0 using its existing data interface (API, database connection, or file export) in read-only mode, without requiring any modification to Sync Matrix 1.0.
generated_requirements.rationale: TC-02 and the Scope section require read-only integration with Sync Matrix 1.0 using its existing interface.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall integrate with Exercise Management Tool 1.0 using its existing data interface (API, database connection, or file export) in read-only mode, without requiring any modification to EMT 1.0.
generated_requirements.rationale: TC-02 and the Scope section require read-only integration with EMT 1.0 using its existing interface.

generated_requirements.text: When a data refresh cycle fails to retrieve data from one or both source applications, the system shall display a clearly visible staleness or connectivity warning indicator to the user, specifying which source(s) are unavailable and the time of the last successful refresh.
generated_requirements.rationale: Failure handling for integration dependencies D-01 and D-02 is critical to prevent users from acting on stale data without awareness.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be deployable as a standalone Windows-based client application on a standard exercise operations center PC, with no dependency on server-side rendering or cloud infrastructure for its core display function.
generated_requirements.rationale: TC-01 constrains the deployment model to a client PC without cloud or server-side rendering dependencies.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow the IT Administrator to manage the list of authorized users, including adding, removing, and modifying user access rights, through an administrative interface or by integration with an external directory service.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-08 and assumption A-04 require the IT Administrator to control user authorization, with identity management maintained externally or via an admin interface.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display a visible indicator showing the current status of the data refresh cycle (e.g., 'Live — Last refreshed at HH:MM:SS', 'Paused', or 'Refresh Failed') at all times within the application window.
generated_requirements.rationale: Users must be continuously aware of data currency to make informed decisions during live exercise execution.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow authorized users to configure display settings — including time segment definitions, filter presets, and layout preferences — and save those configurations for reuse across exercise sessions.
generated_requirements.rationale: The Scope section includes display configuration and modification capabilities, and BR-10 requires per-exercise configurability.

generated_requirements.text: The data displayed in EEMDS shall not exceed 60 seconds of staleness under normal operating conditions, measured from the time a change is committed in a source application to the time it is reflected in the EEMDS display.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Objective 1 defines a maximum data staleness of 60 seconds as a measurable success criterion.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall complete each 60-second data refresh cycle — including data retrieval from both source applications, processing, and display update — without any perceptible degradation in display responsiveness or usability.
generated_requirements.rationale: OC-02 requires that the 60-second refresh cycle does not degrade display performance or usability.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall respond to all user interactions (e.g., applying a filter, switching views, opening a deep link) within 2 seconds under normal operating conditions.
generated_requirements.rationale: Responsive UI interaction is essential during high-tempo exercise execution where controller decision speed is critical.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be available 99% of the time during normal business hours (0800–1800 local time, Monday through Friday) over the first six months of operation; planned maintenance windows shall be scheduled outside of these hours.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Objective 3 and OC-01 define a 99% availability target during normal business hours as a measurable success criterion.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall render all Event and Activity text, color-coded status indicators, and timeline elements at font sizes and visual scales sufficient for 90% of viewers to read Event/Activity detail data from a distance of 30 feet on a 10×10 projection screen.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Objective 8 sets a measurable readability standard of 30-foot legibility on a 10×10 projection screen.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall render color-coded Event status indicators at a visual scale sufficient for 90% of viewers to correctly interpret schedule variance status from a distance of 100 feet on a 10×10 projection screen.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Objective 2 sets a measurable color-code interpretability standard at 100-foot viewing distance on a 10×10 screen.

generated_requirements.text: 90% of new users shall be able to independently initiate an Event or Activity display within 90 minutes of first use, without formal training.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Objective 4 defines a measurable learnability criterion to minimize the learning curve for exercise controllers.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce role-based access control such that 100% of access attempts by unauthorized users are denied and 100% of access attempts by authorized users are granted, with zero unauthorized access incidents.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Objective 7 and BR-08 require perfect enforcement of access control with zero unauthorized access incidents.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall comply with all applicable DHS IT security policies governing user authentication and access control for internal operational tools, as specified in RC-02.
generated_requirements.rationale: RC-02 mandates compliance with DHS IT security policies for authentication and access control.

generated_requirements.text: All user authentication credentials and session tokens shall be transmitted and stored using encryption mechanisms consistent with DHS IT security standards; plaintext credential storage or transmission is prohibited.
generated_requirements.rationale: DHS IT security compliance and the sensitivity of exercise operations require encrypted handling of authentication data.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall terminate an authenticated user session after a configurable period of inactivity, requiring re-authentication before access is restored.
generated_requirements.rationale: Session timeout is a standard DHS IT security control to prevent unauthorized access via unattended authenticated sessions.

generated_requirements.text: The history report shall be tamper-evident; once a change record is written to the history log, it shall not be modifiable or deletable by any user, including administrators.
generated_requirements.rationale: The integrity of the audit trail is essential for reliable after-action review and must be protected against post-hoc alteration.

generated_requirements.text: The history report shall capture change records with sufficient granularity to reconstruct the complete state of all Events and Activities at any point in time during the exercise, including the identity of the source application that originated each change.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Objective 6 requires a complete history report enabling full after-action reconstruction of the exercise timeline.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall recover from a transient data source connectivity failure and automatically resume the 60-second refresh cycle without requiring user intervention, once connectivity to the source application is restored.
generated_requirements.rationale: Automatic recovery from transient failures is necessary to maintain situational awareness continuity during live exercise execution.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall preserve the last successfully retrieved dataset in memory and continue displaying it — with a visible staleness warning — during any period when a source application is unreachable, rather than clearing the display.
generated_requirements.rationale: Displaying the last known good data with a warning is preferable to a blank display during transient source outages.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be maintainable such that updates to the DHS color schema can be applied to all affected UI elements through a centralized configuration file or settings module, without requiring code changes to individual UI components.
generated_requirements.rationale: The DHS color schema may evolve over time; centralized color configuration reduces the cost and risk of future updates.

generated_requirements.text: The integration layer connecting EEMDS to Sync Matrix 1.0 and EMT 1.0 shall be implemented as a modular, independently configurable component so that changes to a source application's data interface can be accommodated without redesigning the core display application.
generated_requirements.rationale: Modular integration components reduce the impact of future source application interface changes on EEMDS.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be deployable on any standard Windows client PC meeting a defined minimum hardware specification, without requiring specialized hardware or proprietary drivers beyond standard display output capabilities.
generated_requirements.rationale: TC-01 requires deployment on a standard client PC, and portability across standard Windows hardware ensures operational flexibility.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall render its display layout correctly at standard projection resolutions (minimum 1920×1080) and shall scale font sizes and graphical elements proportionally when the application window is resized or projected to a 10×10 screen.
generated_requirements.rationale: TC-04 requires correct rendering on projection equipment, and resolution-independent scaling ensures readability across display environments.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide contextual on-screen guidance (e.g., tooltips, labeled controls, and status messages) sufficient to enable a first-time user to navigate core functions — including filtering, pausing refresh, and accessing the full exercise view — without reference to external documentation.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Objective 4 requires 90% of new users to operate the system independently within 90 minutes, necessitating self-explanatory UI design.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall log all user authentication events — including successful logins, failed login attempts, and session terminations — with timestamps and user identifiers, and these logs shall be accessible to the IT Administrator.
generated_requirements.rationale: Authentication event logging supports DHS IT security compliance and enables investigation of any unauthorized access incidents.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display a clear visual distinction between the local time clock and the exercise time clock (e.g., distinct labels, colors, or positional separation) to prevent misidentification during high-stress exercise operations.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-09 requires the two clocks to be visually distinct; misidentification of exercise time versus local time could cause critical decision errors.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support concurrent use by multiple authorized users on separate client PCs within the same exercise operations center network, with each instance independently refreshing data from the source applications.
generated_requirements.rationale: Operations center environments involve multiple controllers and observers who may each run an EEMDS instance simultaneously.

generated_requirements.text: The history report shall be exportable in at least one structured, human-readable format (e.g., CSV or PDF) to support offline after-action review and archival by AAR Analysts.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Objective 6 requires the history report to be available at exercise conclusion; export capability ensures AAR Analysts can work with the data outside the live system.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall validate the structural integrity and completeness of MSEL Summary data received from each source application during every refresh cycle and shall flag any records with missing mandatory fields in the display without crashing or corrupting the existing dataset.
generated_requirements.rationale: Data quality validation during ingestion prevents malformed source records from degrading display accuracy or causing application failures during live exercise execution.

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall refresh the display every 60 seconds.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The application shall match the color of the schema set forth by Department of Homeland Security
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: If projected the data must be readable. On a 10x10 projection screen 90% of viewers must be able to read Event / Activity data from a viewing distance of 30
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall be available during normal business hours. As long as the user has access to the client PC the system will be available 99% of the time during the first six months of operation.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: If projected the data must be understandable. On a 10x10 projection screen 90% of viewers must be able to determine that Events or Activities are occuring in current time from a viewing distance of 100
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall ensure that it can only be accessed by authorized users. The product will be able to distinguish between authorized and unauthorized users in all access attempts
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall be intuitive and self-explanatory. : 90% of new users shall be able to start the display of Events or Activities within 90 minutes of using the product.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall respond fast to keep up-to-date data in the display.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall have a MDI form that allows for the viewing of the graph and the data table.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall display Events in a vertical table by time.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall display the Events in a graph by time.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall display Events or Activities.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The display shall have two regions: left 2/3 of the display is graphical right 1/3 of the display is a data table
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The data displayed in both the nodes within the graph and the rows in the table are MSEL Summary data
ground_truth_requirements.label: NONE

ground_truth_requirements.text: The table side of the display shall be split into 2 regions: sequential and temporal.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The top 1/4 of the table will hold events that are to occur sequentially.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The bottom 3/4 of the table will hold events that occur according to its relevance to current time.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall color code events according to their variance from current time.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall display data from the Sync Matrix 1.0 and Exercise Management Tool 1.0 applications
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall link Events back to either the Sync Matrix 1.0 or the Exercise Managment Tool 1.0 applications for modifications.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall allow modification of the display.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall offer a display of all the Events in the exercise.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall filter data by: Venues and Key Events.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall allow a user to define the time segments
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall display the local and exercise time in separate clocks
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall offer the ability to pause and resume the refresh of data.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall provide charts for the Activity or Event actual versus assumed time.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall provide a history report of changes made to the Activity or Event data
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

generated_requirements.text: The system shall require every user to authenticate with valid credentials (username and password) before granting access to any system function.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-01 mandates that every session begin with authentication; only registered realtors may access the system.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall verify that an authenticated user is a formally registered realtor by checking against the organisation's realtor registry before authorising access to any feature.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-01 requires authorisation in addition to authentication; unregistered individuals must be denied at all entry points.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall deny access and display an appropriate rejection message to any user who fails authentication or is not found in the registered realtor registry.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-01 requires that unregistered individuals be denied access at all entry points with no partial access granted.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow a realtor to search MLS property listings by specifying one or more criteria (e.g., location, price range, property type, number of bedrooms).
generated_requirements.rationale: MLS Query and Search is a core in-scope capability required to eliminate the field productivity gap described in the problem statement.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display MLS search results including at least one clear property image per listing, along with key property details.
generated_requirements.rationale: The BRD scope explicitly requires results to be displayed with clear property images to support on-site client interactions.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall return MLS property search results within 30 seconds of query submission under normal operating conditions, 100% of the time.
generated_requirements.rationale: The BRD success criterion and Section 7.1 define 30 seconds as a hard performance ceiling for MLS search results.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow a realtor to save property search results to local device storage for later review, including in offline mode.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-07 requires that realtors be able to save search results that remain accessible when offline.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow a realtor to create a new property listing in the MLS by entering all required property data fields from the mobile device.
generated_requirements.rationale: MLS Listing Management is explicitly in scope; agents must be able to create listings from the field.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow a realtor to update an existing MLS property listing from the mobile device.
generated_requirements.rationale: MLS Listing Management scope includes updating existing listings to keep MLS data current from the field.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall validate all property listing data entered by the realtor against defined MLS data validation rules before submitting the listing to the MLS, and shall reject and report any malformed or incomplete data to the user.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-02 requires data validation controls to prevent incorrect or malformed data from being introduced into MLS records.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall generate a Comparative Market Analysis (CMA) report based on property criteria explicitly specified by the realtor.
generated_requirements.rationale: CMA Report Generation is a core in-scope capability; BR-08 requires explicit criteria input before generation.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall not generate a CMA report unless the realtor has explicitly entered property criteria; the system shall prompt the user to provide criteria if none are supplied.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-08 explicitly prohibits generating a CMA report without explicit criteria input from the user.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall generate and deliver a completed CMA report to the realtor within 60 seconds of criteria entry.
generated_requirements.rationale: The BRD success criterion and Section 7.1 define 60 seconds as a hard performance ceiling for CMA report generation.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow a realtor to save a generated CMA report to local device storage so that it remains accessible in offline mode.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-04 requires that previously downloaded CMA reports be accessible when internet connectivity is unavailable.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display a map showing the locations of properties in a specified area, using data sourced from the MLS dataset accessible to the authenticated realtor.
generated_requirements.rationale: Property Mapping is in scope; BR-09 restricts map display to properties within the authenticated realtor's accessible MLS dataset.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide turn-by-turn driving directions to a selected property from within the application, without requiring the realtor to use a separate mapping tool.
generated_requirements.rationale: Driving directions are in scope to eliminate the navigation overhead pain point identified in the problem statement.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall restrict the provision of driving directions and property location maps to properties that exist within the MLS dataset accessible to the authenticated realtor.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-09 explicitly limits map and directions functionality to properties within the authenticated realtor's MLS dataset.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall download and display the authenticated realtor's client contacts from the office system, allowing the realtor to view contact details from the mobile device.
generated_requirements.rationale: Contact management is in scope to eliminate data fragmentation and allow field access to client information.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall download and display the authenticated realtor's appointments from the office system, allowing the realtor to view appointment details from the mobile device.
generated_requirements.rationale: Appointment management is in scope; agents must access current appointment data from the field.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically synchronise the realtor's contacts and appointments with the office system once every hour without requiring any action from the realtor.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-03 mandates automatic hourly synchronisation to ensure field data is never more than 60 minutes stale.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall notify the realtor immediately upon detecting, during automatic synchronisation, that a new appointment has been added to their schedule.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-06 requires the realtor to be notified of new appointments discovered during synchronisation without manual checking.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall initiate a call to a seller or buyer, using the smart device's native telephony capability, for the purpose of scheduling an appointment on behalf of the realtor.
generated_requirements.rationale: Appointment Scheduling and Notification scope and BR-10 require the system to be able to call clients to schedule appointments.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall restrict appointment-related calls to contacts that are associated with the authenticated realtor's own client records.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-10 requires that communications be traceable to the realtor's account and limited to their own client contacts.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall notify the realtor immediately when a seller or buyer responds to an appointment request initiated by the system, without requiring the realtor to manually check for updates.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-06 explicitly requires automated notification to the realtor upon client response to an appointment request.

generated_requirements.text: When internet connectivity is unavailable, the system shall automatically switch to offline mode and permit the realtor to read previously downloaded search results, CMA reports, contacts, and appointments.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-04 and the future state description require seamless offline read access to cached data when connectivity is lost.

generated_requirements.text: When operating in offline mode, the system shall restrict the realtor to read-only access and shall not permit write operations (e.g., new listings, listing updates) to be submitted.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-04 specifies that offline mode is read-only; write operations cannot be completed without connectivity.

generated_requirements.text: When operating in offline mode, the system shall queue any write operations attempted by the realtor and automatically submit them to the appropriate backend system upon restoration of internet connectivity.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-04 requires that write operations that cannot be completed offline be queued for submission upon reconnection.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow the realtor to save and manage user preferences, including search filters, display settings, and saved results.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-05 and the User Preference Management scope require the system to persist user-defined preferences across sessions.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall restore 100% of saved user preferences to their last known state following any system failure or connectivity interruption.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-05 and the BRD success criterion require complete preference restoration after any failure event.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a self-service installation process that can be completed by an untrained realtor on a Windows CE or Palm OS device without reference to separately printed instructions.
generated_requirements.rationale: The self-service installation scope and Section 7.2 require installation to be completable without IT assistance or printed materials.

generated_requirements.text: The self-service installation process shall be successfully completable by at least 90% of untrained realtors without external assistance.
generated_requirements.rationale: The BRD success criterion explicitly sets a 90% untrained-success KPI for self-installation as a measurable usability target.

generated_requirements.text: The system user interface shall use terminology, workflows, and navigation patterns natural to the real estate profession so that realtors with no formal product training can use all features without assistance.
generated_requirements.rationale: Section 7.2 and the BRD success criterion require the system to be usable by realtors with no formal training.

generated_requirements.text: The system user interface shall conform to the native UI standards and conventions of the target smart device platform, including standard menus and navigation buttons, for both Windows CE and Palm OS.
generated_requirements.rationale: Section 7.1 constrains the UI to conform to native platform standards to ensure familiarity and ease of use on target devices.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be available 24 hours per day, 365 days per year, achieving a minimum availability rate of 99.9% (no more than 1 failure per 1,000 access attempts).
generated_requirements.rationale: The BRD success criterion and Section 7.1 define 99.9% availability as a hard operational requirement.

generated_requirements.text: The system backend shall support a minimum of 1,000 simultaneous authenticated users at the time of initial launch.
generated_requirements.rationale: The BRD success criterion and Section 7.1 require support for 1,000 concurrent users at launch.

generated_requirements.text: The system architecture shall be designed to scale to support 5,000 simultaneous users within 12 months of launch without requiring a fundamental redesign of the system.
generated_requirements.rationale: The BRD success criterion and Section 7.1 require the architecture to scale fivefold within 12 months to match organisational growth.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce authentication and authorisation on every access attempt, ensuring zero unauthorised access incidents.
generated_requirements.rationale: The BRD success criterion requires 100% of users to be authenticated and authorised before accessing any function, with zero unauthorised access.

generated_requirements.text: All data transmitted between the mobile application and backend systems (office system, MLS, authentication server) shall be encrypted in transit using industry-standard encryption protocols.
generated_requirements.rationale: Security constraints and MLS data governance rules require protection of proprietary MLS and client data during transmission.

generated_requirements.text: User credentials and sensitive client data stored on the device shall be encrypted at rest to prevent unauthorised access if the device is lost or stolen.
generated_requirements.rationale: Security constraints require protection of proprietary data; field devices are at elevated risk of physical loss.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall run natively on Windows CE and Palm OS smart device platforms; no other mobile platforms are in scope for this release.
generated_requirements.rationale: Section 7.1 constrains the platform to Windows CE and Palm OS as the only supported operating systems for this release.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall store contacts, appointments, saved search results, CMA reports, and user preferences in local device storage to support offline operation.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-04, BR-07, and BR-05 collectively require local caching of data to enable offline read access and preference persistence.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall detect the loss of internet connectivity and automatically transition to offline mode, displaying a clear indicator to the realtor that the application is operating offline.
generated_requirements.rationale: Transparent offline/online transition behaviour is required so realtors understand the current operating mode and data currency.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall detect the restoration of internet connectivity and automatically transition back to online mode, triggering submission of any queued write operations and initiating a synchronisation cycle.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-04 requires queued writes to be submitted upon reconnection; seamless transition back to online mode is essential for data integrity.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall log all appointment-related communications (calls to sellers or buyers) initiated by the system, associating each log entry with the realtor's account and the relevant client record.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-10 requires that all system-initiated communications be traceable to the realtor's account for accountability and compliance.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall recover from a crash or unexpected failure and restore the application to a usable state, with all saved user preferences and cached data intact.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-05 and the BRD success criterion require 100% restoration of saved preferences following any system failure.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display a clear, user-friendly error message when an MLS query, CMA generation, or synchronisation operation fails, and shall indicate whether the failure is due to connectivity loss or a backend error.
generated_requirements.rationale: Realtors with no technical training must be able to understand system errors and take appropriate action without IT support.

generated_requirements.text: The system codebase shall be structured and documented to allow maintenance and feature updates to be applied independently for the Windows CE and Palm OS platform variants without requiring a full rebuild of shared business logic.
generated_requirements.rationale: Supporting two legacy platforms requires a maintainable architecture that isolates platform-specific code from shared logic to reduce future maintenance cost.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce that appointment-scheduling calls are only placed to phone numbers present in the authenticated realtor's client contact records, and shall not permit calls to arbitrary numbers.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-10 restricts communications to contacts associated with the realtor's own client records to ensure compliance and prevent misuse.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide the realtor with a visual confirmation and summary upon successful completion of each hourly synchronisation cycle, including the timestamp of the last successful sync.
generated_requirements.rationale: Realtors need to know the currency of their data; displaying the last sync timestamp supports informed decision-making in the field.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall ensure that all appointment-scheduling communications initiated on behalf of realtors comply with applicable telecommunications and consumer contact regulations.
generated_requirements.rationale: Section 7.4 identifies regulatory compliance for consumer communications as a binding constraint on the appointment-calling feature.

ground_truth_requirements.text: The look and feel of the system shall conform to the user interface standards of the smart device.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The user interface shall have standard menus buttons for navigation
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall have a professional appearance
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall have a consistent color scheme and fonts.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall be used by realtors with no training.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall be intuitive and self explanatory.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall be easy for a realtor to learn.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall use symbols and words that are naturally understandable by the realtor community.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall produce search results in an acceptable time
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The search results shall be returned no later 30 seconds after the user has entered the search criteria
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall generate a CMA report in an acceptable time.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The CMA report shall be returned no later 60 seconds after the user has entered the CMA report criteria.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall synchronize contacts and appointments in an acceptable time.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall synchronize with the office system every hour.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall be available for use 24 hours per day 365 days per year
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: Out of 1000 accesses to the system the system is available 999 times.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall operate in offline mode whenever internet connection is unavailable.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall allow the user to view previously downloaded search results CMA reports and appointments.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall retain user preferences in the event of a failure.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: 100% of saved user preferences shall be restored when system comes back online.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall be able to support multiple remote users
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall be able support 1000 simultaneous users.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall be capable of handling the existing 1000 users. This number is expected to grow 5 times within the next year.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall be installed by an untrained realtor without recourse to separately-printed instructions.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: 90% of untrained realtors shall be able to install the product on their device without printed instructions
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product is expected to run on Windows CE and Palm operating systems.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: Only registered realtors shall be able to access the system.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: Every user of the system shall be authenticated and authorized.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall prevent its data from incorrect data being introduced.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall allow a real estate agent to query MLS information
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The user shall be able to get driving directions to a selected property
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The user shall be able to download appointments and contact information for clients
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall display clear property images in the search results
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall update or create new property listings in the MLS
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: Realtor shall be able to generate a CMA report based on property criteria
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall be able to call the seller or buyer to schedule an appointment
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The realtor shall be notified of new client appointments after automatic synchronization with office system
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The realtor shall be able to retrieve a map showing property locations for a specified area
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall notify the realtor when a seller or buyer responds to an appointment request
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall allow the user to save the property search results.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR
